sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Adamas Trust Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Adamas Trust (ADAM) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.23 per share for its common stock, effective for the quarter ending December 31, 2025. Additionally, dividends were announced for several series of its preferred stock. This action reinforces the company's commitment to shareholder returns, making it attractive to income-focused investors, despite a "Neutral" rating from Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, which noted mixed financial performance and high leverage concerns.

feed.title: |Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.88
feed.summary: |Stryker Corporation (NYSE:SYK) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.88 per share, a 4.8% increase from its previous dividend, payable on January 30th to shareholders of record on December 31st. The company has a strong record of 32 consecutive years of dividend increases and a low payout ratio of 22.6%, indicating solid coverage by earnings. Despite recent insider selling totaling approximately $185.4 million, analysts anticipate the medical technology company will post an EPS of $13.47 for the current year.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. Declares Fourth Quarter Dividend of $1.56 Per Share
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (MPWR) has declared a fourth-quarter dividend of $1.56 per share for stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025, with payment scheduled for January 15, 2026. The announcement highlights the company's financial stability and commitment to investor returns, despite a safe harbor statement cautioning against over-reliance on forward-looking statements. Insider trading data shows significant sales by executives, while institutional investors show mixed activity in MPWR stock.

feed.title: How Amazon is supporting flood-impacted communities in Washington state
feed.summary: Amazon is actively supporting communities affected by historic flooding in Washington state, mobilizing its disaster relief services. The company is prioritizing the safety of its employees and partners, and is delivering essential supplies through its Disaster Relief Hubs in partnership with organizations like the American Red Cross and Save the Children. These efforts include providing hygiene kits, comfort items for children, and medical devices to shelters in western Washington.

feed.title: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) Stock Price Up 1.3% After Analyst Upgrade
feed.summary: Merck & Co., Inc. (NYSE:MRK) saw its stock price rise by 1.3% after Morgan Stanley increased its price target from $100 to $102, maintaining an "equal-weight" rating. Several other brokers also boosted their targets, contributing to a consensus "Hold" rating with an average target price of $107.50. The company recently raised its quarterly dividend to $0.85 and exceeded quarterly EPS estimates.

feed.title: AI boosts customer service jobs, not cuts: Gartner survey
feed.summary: A Gartner survey reveals that AI is augmenting customer service roles rather than replacing them, with only 20% of organizations reducing headcount due to AI. The study found that 55% of firms maintain stable staffing while handling increased customer volumes, using AI to boost efficiency and optimize workflows. AI is also creating new specialized positions like AI strategists and conversational AI designers, suggesting a catalyst for workforce evolution in customer service and outsourcing.

feed.title: Global payments EVP Sheffield sells $164k in stock
feed.summary: David M Sheffield, EVP and Chief Accounting Officer at Global Payments Inc., sold 2,016 shares of company stock for $164,122. This transaction comes amidst the company's planned acquisition of Worldpay Holdco, LLC and a new partnership with Uber Eats. Several analysts have also raised their price targets for Global Payments, reflecting positive strategic developments.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials chief strategy officer Stanley G. Bass to retire in April 2026
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials Company announced that Stanley G. Bass, its chief strategy officer, will retire on April 30, 2026, as per a recent SEC filing. The company is currently valued at $39.15 billion with a 17.21% year-to-date return, and Bass's compensation structure will remain unchanged until his retirement. This announcement follows strong Q3 2025 financial results and positive analyst ratings for the construction materials company.

feed.title: Union Pacific appoints CF Industries CEO Tony Will to board of directors
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation has announced the appointment of W. Anthony "Tony" Will, current President and CEO of CF Industries Holdings, to its Board of Directors, effective January 6, 2026. Will, who is set to retire from CF Industries on January 4, 2026, brings extensive leadership experience to Union Pacific. The railroad company, valued at $142 billion, has a strong financial health rating and a history of consistent dividend payments.

feed.title: OXY: UBS Maintains Neutral Rating, Lowers Price Target to $43
feed.summary: UBS analyst Josh Silverstein maintained a Neutral rating on Occidental Petroleum (OXY) and lowered the price target from $45 to $43. This adjustment reflects a 4.44% decrease in projected stock value. The consensus among 27 brokerage firms indicates a "Hold" status for OXY, with an average target price of $49.68 from 24 analysts.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands Insider Sold Shares Worth $26,567,002, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Las Vegas Sands Corp. (NYSE: LVS) recently sold shares totalling $26,567,002, as reported in a recent SEC filing. This transaction follows several other insider share sales from the company throughout November and December, alongside various analyst price target adjustments for Las Vegas Sands.

feed.title: Las Vegas Sands CEO Goldstein sells $26.6 million in stock By Investing.com
feed.summary: Las Vegas Sands (NASDAQ:LVS) Chairman and CEO Robert G. Goldstein sold 399,900 shares of common stock for approximately $26.6 million on December 10 and 11, 2025. These sales occurred after Goldstein exercised options for the same number of shares. Following these transactions, Goldstein directly holds 100 shares and indirectly holds 129,005 shares, while the company maintains a "GREAT" financial health score, impressive gross profit margins, and has received positive updates from several financial institutions.

feed.title: This GE Spinoff Is Beating the S&P 500, Nvidia—and Bitcoin. Here’s How.
feed.summary: GE Vernova, a spinoff from General Electric, is outperforming the S&P 500, Nvidia, and Bitcoin by delivering gas turbines at high prices with a substantial backlog. The article highlights how the three-way split of the former General Electric has significantly benefited investors. The author notes that while the success might not have been entirely according to plan, the financial results speak for themselves.

feed.title: Alector (ALEC) Appoints Neil Berkley as CFO
feed.summary: Alector (ALEC) has appointed Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer, effective December 10, 2025, while he will also retain his responsibilities as Chief Business Officer. The biotechnology company, which focuses on neurodegenerative therapies, faces significant financial challenges including negative margins and declining revenue growth. Despite a stable liquidity position, an Altman Z-Score of -3.61 indicates potential bankruptcy risk, and recent insider activity shows substantial selling with no buying.

feed.title: Closing Talent Gaps Requires Addressing Root Causes: 3M
feed.summary: 3M is addressing talent gaps, particularly in STEM and manufacturing, through its "25 Women in Science 2026 – Women in Manufacturing Edition" program. This initiative aims to elevate women in scientific and industrial roles, counteracting early-stage stereotypes and cultural barriers that limit their participation. By creating visible role models and collaborating with various stakeholders, 3M seeks to strengthen the pipeline of female talent and support Mexico's transition towards innovation and advanced industry capabilities.

feed.title: Skyworks Solutions approves executive incentive plan for fiscal 2026 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc. (NASDAQ: SWKS) has approved its Fiscal Year 2026 Executive Incentive Plan, which offers cash incentive awards to senior management based on achieving corporate performance metrics like revenue and non-GAAP operating income targets. The plan details potential award percentages of base salary for various executive roles, with the possibility of doubling rewards for exceeding targets. This announcement follows recent financial results where Skyworks surpassed revenue forecasts and met EPS expectations, though its outlook for the mobile segment is mixed.

feed.title: Food Waste Recycling Machines Market and Companies Analysis Report 2019-2031 Featuring BioHiTech Global, Emerson Electric, KCS Engineering, Bhor Engineering, WISErg, IMC Waste Station and More - ResearchAndMarkets.com
feed.summary: The global food waste recycling machine market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 6.6% from 2025 to 2031, driven by increasing food waste, environmental concerns, and government support. Key trends include smart and automated systems, decentralized processing, and waste-to-energy solutions. The market also sees growth in advanced composting, digitalization integration, and diversification of end products, with APAC expected to show the highest regional growth.

feed.title: Union Pacific appoints CF Industries CEO Tony Will to board of directors
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation has announced the appointment of W. Anthony "Tony" Will, CEO of CF Industries Holdings, to its Board of Directors, effective January 6, 2026. Will, who has led CF Industries since 2014 and plans to retire from the company on January 4, 2026, brings extensive leadership experience to Union Pacific. This move is part of the railroad giant's strategy to strengthen its leadership team, following a strong third quarter and significant shareholder approval for a merger with Norfolk Southern.

feed.title: Invitation Homes increases quarterly dividend by 3.4% to $0.30
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (NYSE:INVH) has announced an increase in its quarterly cash dividend by 3.4% to $0.30 per share on its common stock, payable by January 16, 2026. This decision reflects the company's consistent dividend growth and comes despite its stock trading near a 52-week low. The announcement follows strong Q3 2025 earnings which surpassed analyst expectations, though Oppenheimer has slightly lowered its price target due to concerns about future rent growth.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares (NASDAQ: HBAN) declares $0.35625 Series I preferred dividend
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares (Nasdaq: HBANM) has declared a quarterly cash dividend on its 5.70% Series I Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock. The dividend amounts to $356.25 per share, equivalent to $0.35625 per depositary share. It is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2026.

feed.title: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated Declares Cash Dividend On Its Series I Preferred Stock
feed.summary: Huntington Bancshares Incorporated has announced a quarterly cash dividend of $356.25 per share ($0.35625 per depositary share) on its 5.70% Series I Non-Cumulative Perpetual Preferred Stock (Nasdaq: HBANM). This dividend is payable on March 2, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 15, 2026. Huntington is a regional bank holding company with $223 billion in assets, providing a wide range of financial services.

feed.title: Advisor moves: Raymond James welcomes $692M advisor team in new bank relationship
feed.summary: Raymond James' Financial Institutions Division has partnered with Bank Midwest, integrating the bank's investment program as Midwest Wealth Group and overseeing $692 million in assets. Concurrently, Ameriprise Financial expanded its Kuttin Wealth Management practice by adding four advisors managing nearly $300 million, while Commonwealth Financial Group's Highland Peak Wealth brought on two managing directors from Citi Private Bank and BNY Wealth. These moves highlight the competitive landscape for client assets and top talent in the financial advisory sector.

feed.title: McDonald’s Brings New Scrutiny to Franchisee Pricing in Move to Emphasize Value
feed.summary: McDonald's is implementing new global franchising standards effective January 1, 2026, to ensure franchisees maintain strong value offerings. While franchisees will continue to set their own prices, McDonald's will assess these decisions to reinforce accountability for value leadership across its 44,000+ global restaurants. This move aims to appeal to customers who expect strong value from the brand and follows a period of significant growth for the company.

feed.title: Invitation Homes Raises Quarterly Dividend to $0.30 a Share From $0.29, Payable Jan. 16 to Holders of Record Dec. 23
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. has announced an increase in its quarterly dividend, raising it to $0.30 per share from the previous $0.29. This updated dividend will be payable on January 16 to shareholders who are on record as of December 23. This news highlights the company's commitment to returning value to its shareholders.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems announces $1.56 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (NASDAQ:MPWR) has declared a quarterly dividend of $1.56 per share, maintaining 12 consecutive years of dividend payments with notable growth. The dividend is payable on January 15, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025. The company, a fabless global provider of semiconductor-based power electronics, has shown strong financial performance with a market capitalization of $45.31 billion and significant stock appreciation year-to-date.

feed.title: Chubb lays out digital transformation plan
feed.summary: Chubb's December 2025 investor presentation details a multi-year digital transformation aimed at structurally resetting operations through automation, data, and AI. The plan anticipates reducing headcount by 20% and generating significant expense savings over the next three to four years by redesigning core processes across underwriting, claims, and support functions. Chubb expects to automate 85% of major underwriting and claims processes and impact approximately 70% of its organization.

feed.title: Invitation Homes Announces Cash Dividend
feed.summary: Invitation Homes Inc. (NYSE: INVH) has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.30 per share, marking a 3.4% increase from the previous dividend. This dividend will be paid on or before January 16, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 23, 2025. Invitation Homes is a premier single-family home leasing and management company.

feed.title: Union Pacific (NYSE: UNP) appoints CF Industries CEO Tony Will to board Jan. 6, 2026
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation announced the appointment of W. Anthony "Tony" Will, current president and CEO of CF Industries Holdings, to its Board of Directors, effective January 6, 2026. Will, who plans to retire from CF Industries on January 4, 2026, will bring significant leadership experience to Union Pacific. His appointment is expected to aid Union Pacific in its ongoing efforts to enhance America's rail infrastructure and supply chain.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Group (NASDAQ:TROW) VP Sells $434,349.60 in Stock
feed.summary: T. Rowe Price Group VP Arif Husain sold 4,260 shares of the company's stock for $434,349.60, reducing his stake by 8.29%. The company recently announced a quarterly dividend of $1.27 per share, yielding 4.8%, and reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst estimates. Analysts currently have a consensus "Reduce" rating on TROW with an average price target of $108.77.

feed.title: Alector, Inc. Appoints Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer
feed.summary: Alector, Inc. has appointed Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer, effective December 10, 2025. Berkley, who served as Chief Business Officer and Interim CFO, will retain his CBO role. CEO Arnon Rosenthal praised Berkley's leadership and extensive biotech experience as crucial for Alector's strategic advancements in neurodegeneration therapies.

feed.title: KROGER CO SEC 10-Q Report
feed.summary: Kroger Co. released its Form 10-Q for Q3 2025, showing sales of $33.859 billion and a net loss of $1.320 billion due to significant impairment charges related to its fulfillment network. Despite the net loss, adjusted net earnings per share increased, and cash flow from operations rose by 6%. The company aims for eCommerce profitability in 2026 after optimizing its fulfillment strategy.

feed.title: Lithium Royalty Corp. Applauds Power Metals’ Strategic Offtake Agreement with Albemarle
feed.summary: Lithium Royalty Corp. (LRC) has celebrated Power Metals Corp.'s off-take agreement with Albemarle Corporation for cesium concentrate, which includes a C$5 million prepayment. This arrangement helps Power Metals achieve its 2026 production target at the Case Lake project, where LRC holds a 2.0% royalty. The project is significant for the limited global cesium market and positions Power Metals as a potential near-term revenue generator for LRC.

feed.title: Texas to invest $14 million as Southwest plans new Austin crew base
feed.summary: Texas Governor Greg Abbott announced a $14 million state investment to support Southwest Airlines' new Austin crew base and flight attendant training facility, expected to create over 2,000 jobs. This follows the city's approval of a $5.5 million incentive package for the airline. The combined incentives totaling $19.5 million solidified Southwest's decision to expand in Austin, with the new crew base set to launch in March 2026.

feed.title: Improving research efficiency with high-performance flow cytometry
feed.summary: This article examines the capabilities of the ZE5 Cell Analyzer, a high-parameter flow cytometer, for enhancing research efficiency. It demonstrates how the instrument delivers high-quality data at an accelerated rate in both bead- and cell-based assays, while maintaining resolution and minimizing carryover. The research highlights the ZE5 Cell Analyzer's potential to meet user demand, expand research scope, and free researchers from lengthy data collection periods.

feed.title: Alector Appoints Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer and Chief Business Officer
feed.summary: Alector, Inc., a clinical-stage biotechnology company, has appointed Neil Berkley as its Chief Financial Officer, effective December 10, 2025. Mr. Berkley will also continue to serve as Chief Business Officer, a role he has held since March 2024 (and interim CFO since June 2025). The company highlighted his exceptional leadership, strategic insight, and deep understanding of their science and business as key qualifications for his expanded role.

feed.title: Barclays Maintains 'Underweight' Rating on T. Rowe Price Group (TROW), Slightly Raises Price Target | TROW Stock News
feed.summary: Barclays analyst Benjamin Budish has reaffirmed an 'Underweight' rating for T. Rowe Price Group (TROW) while making a slight upward adjustment to the price target from $101.00 to $102.00. This adjustment represents a modest 0.99% increase, with the average target price from 12 analysts being $107.25 suggesting a 2.54% upside from the current price. T. Rowe Price Group provides asset management services and primarily manages retirement-based accounts, giving it a stable client base.

feed.title: Better Home & Finance (NASDAQ:BETR) Trading Down 8.7% - Time to Sell?
feed.summary: Better Home & Finance (NASDAQ:BETR) experienced an 8.7% decline, trading down to $46.20 on lighter volume. Analyst sentiment is mixed-to-negative with a "Reduce" consensus, while significant insider selling, totaling over $51 million in the last 90 days, raises concerns despite insiders still owning 23.59% of the stock. Institutional investors have made various adjustments to their holdings, with Vanguard Group Inc. increasing its position slightly.

feed.title: Barings Participation Investors declares $0.37 quarterly dividend By Investing.com
feed.summary: Barings Participation Investors (NYSE:MPV) has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.37 per share, payable on January 16, 2026, to shareholders of record on December 30, 2025. The company currently offers a substantial 7.46% dividend yield with annual payments totaling $1.48 per share. The dividend is expected to consist of net investment income, with final tax characteristics determined after the fiscal year-end.

feed.title: Alector (NASDAQ: ALEC) appoints Neil Berkley as CFO while retaining chief business officer role
feed.summary: Alector (NASDAQ: ALEC) has appointed Neil Berkley as Chief Financial Officer, effective December 10, 2025, while he also continues in his role as Chief Business Officer. Berkley brings over two decades of experience in corporate strategy, finance, and business development to the position, having served as Interim CFO since June 2025. This appointment consolidates leadership as Alector navigates a strategic refocusing period following a recent failed Phase 3 trial and workforce reduction.

feed.title: Transcontinental Realty Investors elects directors and ratifies auditor at annual meeting By Investing.com
feed.summary: Transcontinental Realty Investors, Inc. (NYSE:TCI) re-elected all incumbent directors and ratified Farmer, Fuqua & Huff, P.C. as its independent auditor during its annual meeting of stockholders on Wednesday. A quorum was established with proxies representing 96.9% of outstanding shares. The article also notes that the board of directors re-elected Henry A. Butler as Chairman and Ted R. Munselle as Presiding Director.

feed.title: Law professor sues Boeing, alleging he breathed in toxic fumes on a Delta flight
feed.summary: A law professor is suing Boeing for $40 million, alleging he suffered severe health issues after inhaling toxic fumes during a Delta flight in August 2024. The lawsuit claims that contaminated cabin air, which smelled like "dirty socks," caused symptoms including dizziness, nausea, and memory loss, as well as mental anguish and lost wages. Boeing has not commented on the suit, while Delta declined to comment but is replacing auxiliary power units on some Airbus aircraft to address similar fume incidents.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group Announces Earnings Release Date
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH) will release its full year 2025 financial results and provide 2026 financial guidance on Tuesday, January 27, 2026, before the market opens. A teleconference with analysts and investors will follow at 8:00 a.m. ET and will be webcast on the company’s investor relations website. The replay will be available through February 10 on the website.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Dividend
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (Nasdaq: MPWR) has declared its fourth-quarter dividend of $1.56 per share of common stock. Stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025, will receive the dividend, which is scheduled for payment on January 15, 2026. The company, a global provider of high-performance, semiconductor-based power electronics solutions, included a safe harbor statement regarding forward-looking statements in its announcement.

feed.title: Water board proposes a $600K fine against Boeing Co.
feed.summary: The Los Angeles Regional Water Quality Control Board has proposed a $600,000 fine against Boeing Co. for nearly 40 stormwater discharge violations at the Santa Susana Field Laboratory. This proposed fine comes as environmental groups are simultaneously pursuing a lawsuit challenging the state’s cleanup agreement for the contaminated site. Boeing maintains high compliance with discharge permits but faces pressure from advocates demanding a full cleanup.

feed.title: APPLIED MATERIALS INC /DE SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: Applied Materials Inc. released its 2025 10-K report, revealing a 4% increase in net revenue to $28,368 million and a 5% increase in operating income to $8,289 million, despite a slight decrease in net income due to higher tax provisions. The report highlights strong performance in its Semiconductor Systems segment, particularly in Asia Pacific, and strategic initiatives focused on R&D, a shift to a subscription model for its AGS segment, and significant share repurchases. The company anticipates continued growth driven by AI and IoT, while also addressing challenges like market cyclicality, supply chain disruptions, and evolving trade regulations.

feed.title: Blades-in turbine inspections at Quad Cities set new benchmark for Constellation
feed.summary: Constellation has set a new benchmark for turbine inspections at its Quad Cities boiling water reactor plants by implementing an innovative "blades-in" phased array ultrasonic testing (PAUT) method. This technique allows for thorough inspection of turbine blades without removal, significantly reducing outage time and costs while ensuring structural integrity. The successful application at Quad Cities-2 and other plants has already yielded substantial savings and industry recognition.

feed.title: Texas Pacific Land stock hits 52-week low at $837.60 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Texas Pacific Land Corp (TPL) stock has fallen to a 52-week low of $837.60, representing a 29.41% decrease over the past year and a 58% drop from its 52-week high. Despite impressive gross profit margins of 94.13% and strong financial health, the company faces challenges, with Q3 2025 earnings and revenue missing analyst expectations. KeyBanc initiated coverage with an Overweight rating and a $1,050 price target, highlighting TPL's significant land and royalty interests in Texas.

feed.title: Monolithic Power Systems Announces Fourth Quarter 2025 Dividend
feed.summary: Monolithic Power Systems, Inc. (Nasdaq: MPWR) announced a fourth-quarter dividend of $1.56 per share of common stock. This dividend will be paid on January 15, 2026, to stockholders of record as of December 31, 2025. The announcement includes a safe harbor statement regarding forward-looking statements.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH) to post full-year 2025 results, 2026 outlook on Jan. 27
feed.summary: UnitedHealth Group (NYSE: UNH) announced it will release its full-year 2025 financial results and provide 2026 guidance on January 27, 2026, before the market opens. Following the release, the company will host a teleconference with analysts and investors at 8:00 a.m. ET. The webcast and replay will be available on the Investor Relations page of UnitedHealth Group’s website.

feed.title: ServiceNow Agrees To Buy Veza To Govern AI Agent Permissions At Scale
feed.summary: ServiceNow announced its intent to acquire Veza for over $1 billion to enhance identity governance for AI agents at scale. This acquisition, following ServiceNow's $2.85 billion deal for Moveworks, aims to integrate Veza's identity security platform with ServiceNow's AI Control Tower to provide critical governance for non-human identities, especially as enterprise AI adoption accelerates. The move signals ServiceNow's strategy to bolster its competitive position in the rapidly evolving AI landscape, addressing challenges like context-dependent permissions and the growing attack surface created by AI agents.
